definition: The SQLite parser must receive the exact 6-byte keyword token 'PRAGMA' at the start of the input (offset 0x00) so the tokenizer emits the PRAGMA token and the SQL grammar dispatches into sqlite3Pragma / pragmaLocate; I2S substitutes the exact 'PRAGMA' operand from the CmpLogObserver buffer while VP's CMP_MAP prefix-distance gradient preserves inputs whose keyword prefix is incrementally closer to 'PRAGMA' as corpus stepping stones, both being jointly necessary because the keyword is a contiguous multi-byte literal whose exact bytes I2S alone can plant but whose corpus scaffold VP must maintain for the downstream pragma-name comparisons to converge.

authoring_contract.goal: ONE parameterized template.c isolating this cluster's shared mechanism, with exactly ONE compile-time -D knob = the program-feature axis. params.json:parameter MUST name that macro; scan_values sweep it; fuzzers = involved_fuzzers; expected_direction = '<winner> > <loser>'.
authoring_contract.isolate: No partial-match coverage edges unless the mechanism IS a gradient (VP families). Keep the gate the only objective.

signature.mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP gradient feedback preserves inputs whose SQL keyword/identifier bytes are progressively closer to matching the parser's compare targets; this lets vpc incrementally accrete a well-formed PRAGMA token where cmplog's edge-only feedback discards near-miss fragments.
signature.one_line: VP gradient preserves partial-PRAGMA keyword inputs toward well-formed token

analysis.summary_one_line: Take the false side of sqlite3NameFromToken by parsing a well-formed PRAGMA/ALTER-RENAME SQL string with a non-error token sequence

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A byte string that incrementally satisfies the SQL keyword/token comparisons feeding sqlite3NameFromToken, e.g. a near-complete 'PRAGMA <name>;' form where multi-byte keyword/identifier compares are progressively closer to matching.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner seeds begin with the exact ASCII keyword 'PRAGMA' (50 52 41 47 4d 41) and a structured identifier/terminator tail, a token sequence the parser accepts so the blocker takes its false (resolving) side.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog loser seeds are short malformed fragments ('SELEEC', '""/""') that fail keyword compares early; without the Hamming/prefix-distance CMP_MAP gradient, cmplog cannot retain and refine inputs that are only partially closer to a valid keyword, so it stays blocked 10/10.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient feedback preserves inputs whose CMP operands are closer to matching the SQL keyword/identifier comparisons; this gradient is the delta cmplog lacks, letting vpc climb toward the well-formed token stream that reaches the false branch.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker sits in sqlite3NameFromToken, reached only through the SQL parser via the ALTER RENAME / PRAGMA path.
The blocker's resolving side is the false branch, globally blocked.
Winner-resolving seeds begin with the exact ASCII 'PRAGMA' keyword bytes.
A second winner seed also leads with 'PRAGMA' followed by an identifier.
cmplog loser seeds are short malformed fragments that fail keyword comparisons.
value_profile loser seeds are corrupt keyword fragments lacking the exact literal.
The winner side leads with 'P' 'R' 'A' at the first offsets while losers carry semicolons and error tokens there.
value_profile preserves inputs whose CMP operands are closer to matching, a gradient cmplog lacks.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile has no I2SRandReplace mutator.
I2SRandReplace substitutes the matched CMP operand into the input at the matching offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: Only value_profile_cmplog resolves the branch; cmplog and value_profile alone stay blocked 10/10.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: value_profile_cmplog             9        1          0  winner (value_profile vs cmplog); winner (I2S vs value_profile)

evidence_trail.claim: The winning vpc seed's ancestor chain contains an I2S-floor dash row indicating an I2SRandReplace find.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: indicate an I2SRandReplace find under the current build

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where the SQL keyword gate feeding sqlite3NameFromToken is resolved by cmplog or value_profile alone at the same rate as value_profile_cmplog, showing neither I2S substitution nor the CMP_MAP gradient is individually necessary.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The CMP_MAP-gradient attribution for the vpc>cmplog pair is inferred from seed shape and mechanism text, not directly verified in lineage (the dash-row signal evidences I2S, not the gradient).

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes the matched SQL keyword operand (e.g. 'PRAGMA') into the input at the matching offset; the winning seed's lineage contains an I2S-floor ancestor, confirming this substitution that value_profile lacks.
signature.one_line: I2S substitutes exact 'PRAGMA' keyword literal into parser input

analysis.summary_one_line: Take the false side of sqlite3NameFromToken by parsing a well-formed PRAGMA/ALTER-RENAME SQL string with a non-error token sequence

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The literal SQL keyword bytes (e.g. 'PRAGMA') and structural punctuation that the lexer/parser compares against fixed string constants, placed at the offsets the comparisons read.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner seeds carry the exact 'PRAGMA' literal plus a trailing identifier ('Ostor', 'vdbe_'); the winning vpc seed's ancestor chain contains an I2S-floor dash row, evidence that I2SRandReplace substituted a matched CMP operand into the input to produce the keyword.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: value_profile loser seeds are corrupt keyword fragments ('ac 45 4c 45 ...ELE', repeated ';;' runs) lacking the exact literal; without I2SRandReplace, value_profile cannot copy the parser's expected string constant directly into the input and stays blocked 10/10.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes the comparison's other operand (the expected SQL keyword literal) into the input at the matched offset; this I2S substitution is the delta value_profile lacks, supplying the exact keyword bytes that drive the parser to the false branch.

signature.mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP gradient (Hamming/prefix-distance buckets on sqlite3StrICmp operand comparisons) preserves corpus inputs whose pragma-name token bytes get incrementally closer to a real table entry; cmplog lacks this distance feedback and so never retains near-match names.
signature.one_line: VP gradient incrementally converges pragma-name token toward real table entry

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must be a PRAGMA statement whose pragma-name token matches a known pragma so pragmaLocate's string-compare loop falls through to the false branch.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A PRAGMA statement whose name-token byte sequence progressively approaches a real pragma name in the case-insensitive name table scanned by pragmaLocate (offsets 0x00-0x08 carrying 'PRAGMA ' plus a pragma-name prefix).
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The vpc winner seed is the well-formed string 'PRAGMA Ostor;-' (offsets 0x00-0x06 = 50 52 41 47 4d 41 20), driving pragmaLocate's sqlite3StrICmp name comparisons toward a match; vpc carries the CMP_MAP gradient feedback that rewards inputs whose name bytes get closer to a table entry.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The cmplog loser has I2S but no CMP_MAP gradient, so it gets no partial-progress reward as a candidate pragma name converges; its blocking seeds are non-PRAGMA garbage like 'SELEEC' and '""/""' that never reach the pragma-name compare loop, and it blocks all 10 trials.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient (Hamming/prefix-distance buckets on the sqlite3StrICmp operand comparisons) preserves corpus inputs whose pragma-name token gets incrementally closer to a real table name; cmplog lacks this distance feedback, so it is the value_profile delta that separates vpc from cmplog.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the false side of a branch inside pragmaLocate, globally blocked.
Only value_profile_cmplog resolves the branch; both cmplog and value_profile block all 10 trials, making this a joint-necessity (synergy) case.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: value_profile_cmplog             8        2          0  winner (value_profile vs cmplog); winner (I2S vs value_profile)

evidence_trail.claim: There are two decisive pairs with different technique deltas (value_profile and I2S), supporting a multi_feature decomposition.
The second decisive pair isolates the I2S technique delta.
The winner-resolving seed is a well-formed PRAGMA statement whose name token begins at offset 0x00.
The loser-blocking seeds are non-PRAGMA malformed inputs that never form a valid pragma name.
A value_profile loser-blocking seed is degenerate punctuation noise, not a pragma name.
The pragma-name resolution path exercises case-insensitive string compare (sqlite3StrICmp), which is the operand the I2S and CMP_MAP feedback act on.
value_profile's distinguishing feedback is a CMP_MAP keyed by operand-distance buckets, rewarding inputs that get closer to matching.

adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

value_profile_cmplog unions both techniques: CmpLogObserver buffer and CMP_MAP gradient buckets.

per-execution CMP buffer (`CmpLogObserver`), and CMP_MAP gradient buckets.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver buffer at the sqlite3StrICmp call and substitutes the exact expected pragma-name bytes into the input; the winning seed's ancestry carries an I2S-floor dash row at depth 6 confirming this substitution, which value_profile lacks.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The exact pragma-name token bytes that equal a real entry in pragmaLocate's name table, so the StrICmp returns equal and the loop exits via the false (non-blocked-globally) path.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The vpc winner's ancestor chain contains an I2S-floor (dash) row at depth 6, evidence that I2SRandReplace substituted the compared pragma-name operand into the input; vpc has the CmpLogObserver buffer that lets I2S copy the literal expected name bytes into the 'PRAGMA ' token region.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: value_profile has the CMP_MAP gradient but no I2SRandReplace stage, so it can only inch toward the name byte-by-byte and never lands the exact multi-byte token; it blocks 10/10 trials and its seeds (e.g. ';;\;;E<' and '.ELE.EL@E..CT') never form a valid pragma name.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver operand recorded at the sqlite3StrICmp call and substitutes the exact expected pragma-name bytes into the input; value_profile lacks I2S, so the I2S delta is what separates vpc from value_profile.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver buffer for the 'PRAGMA' keyword comparison and substitutes those exact bytes at offset 0x00; the winning seed's lineage carries an I2S-floor dash row at depth 6, confirming the I2S contribution that value_profile (no CmpLog) cannot provide.
signature.one_line: I2S substitutes 'PRAGMA' keyword at offset 0 driving sqlite3Pragma dispatch

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must begin with the literal SQL keyword 'PRAGMA' to enter sqlite3Pragma's pragma-name dispatch and take the true branch.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The input must carry the exact ASCII token 'PRAGMA' at offset 0x00 (bytes 50 52 41 47 4d 41) so the parser dispatches into sqlite3Pragma and reaches the pragma-name comparison at 125998:7.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The winner-resolving seed begins '50 52 41 47 4d 41 20 4f 73 74 6f 72' = 'PRAGMA Ostor', presenting the verbatim PRAGMA keyword that the loser seeds (all SELECT/junk) never form.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Every loser-blocking seed is a SELECT-like or junk byte run ('SELEEC', '""/""', '.ELE.EL@E..CT') with no 'PRAGMA' token, so the pragma-name branch at 125998 is never entered (L shows T=0 F=20).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: Against value_profile (Pair 2 delta=I2S), only the winner has I2SRandReplace, which reads the CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes the program's 'PRAGMA' keyword operand into the input at matching offsets; the winning seed's lineage carries an I2S-floor dash row at depth 6, evidence of an I2S find in its ancestry.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the true side of a branch at sqlite3.c:125998 inside sqlite3Pragma.
The globally blocked side is the true branch.
The winner-resolving seed begins with the literal ASCII keyword 'PRAGMA' at offset 0x00.
Loser-blocking seeds are SELECT-like / junk byte runs with no PRAGMA token.
At the blocker line the winner takes the true side once while the loser never does (T=0 across 20).
The winner-resolving offsets 0x00-0x05 hold the PRAGMA bytes while the loser sets at those offsets are dominated by ';', 'E', 'C', 'S' with no matching keyword.
cmplog's I2SRandReplace substitutes a logged CMP operand into the input at matching offsets, which can paste the program's PRAGMA keyword.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating the true branch on the literal 'PRAGMA' keyword where value_profile (no I2S, CMP_MAP only) and cmplog (I2S, no CMP_MAP) each reach the branch as readily as value_profile_cmplog, showing neither technique is necessary.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: Whether the divergence is genuine joint-necessity of I2S and CMP_MAP versus a single dominant technique is not separable from a single winner-resolving seed plus a 1-dash-row floor signal.

signature.mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP writes a Hamming/prefix-distance bucket for each keyword/string CMP, so an input whose prefix is closer to 'PRAGMA' than any previous is kept as corpus; this graded prefix-climbing is absent from cmplog's edge-only feedback.
signature.one_line: VP gradient climbs prefix distance toward 'PRAGMA' keyword through edge feedback

hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The winner-resolving seed 'PRAGMA Ostor;-...' supplies the full keyword and reaches the branch (W shows T=1 F=1 at L125998), while cmplog's corpus stalls without it.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog loser seeds ('SELEEC .`', '""/"" ') prefix-collide on no more than a couple of bytes with 'PRAGMA' and never accrete the full keyword, so they keep taking the false side (loser resolved=0/10, blocked=10).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: Against cmplog (Pair 1 delta=value_profile), only the winner adds the CMP_MAP gradient: each integer/string CMP writes a Hamming/prefix-distance bucket so an input whose prefix is closer to 'PRAGMA' than any seen before is preserved as corpus, giving a graded climb toward the keyword that plain cmplog edge-feedback lacks.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace reads the per-execution CMP buffer and substitutes 'PRAGMA' keyword bytes at the input head, causing the tokenizer to emit the PRAGMA token; the winning seed's lineage contains an I2S-floor ancestor confirming this contribution, absent from value_profile.
signature.one_line: I2S plants 'PRAGMA' keyword so tokenizer emits token driving yy_reduce case

analysis.summary_one_line: Reaching this yy_reduce PRAGMA reduce action needs a literal 'PRAGMA' keyword token, requiring BOTH I2S substitution and CMP_MAP gradient.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The input must begin with the exact SQL keyword bytes 'PRAGMA' (50 52 41 47 4d 41) so the SQLite tokenizer emits the PRAGMA token that drives yy_reduce to this reduce action at 157969.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Both winner-resolving seeds carry the literal ASCII 'PRAGMA' at offsets 0x00-0x05 (PRAGMA Ostor / PRAGMA vdbe_), and only the winner takes the true branch (W T=2 F=17 vs L T=0 F=135 at L157969).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: value_profile-discovered loser seeds are SELECT-family / punctuation garbage (e.g. ELE...CT, ;;\;;E<) with no contiguous PRAGMA keyword, so the tokenizer never emits the PRAGMA token and the true side stays at T=0.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: Adding I2S (cmplog's I2SRandReplace) to value_profile lets the mutator read the per-execution CMP buffer and substitute the keyword-matching bytes for 'PRAGMA' at the input head; the winning vpc seed's ancestor chain contains an I2S-floor dash row, confirming an I2S contribution that value_profile alone lacks.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the true side of a branch at sqlite3.c:157969 inside the parser reduce function yy_reduce.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating only on a literal PRAGMA keyword that value_profile_cmplog resolves but where cmplog-alone (with I2S) also resolves it at the same rate, showing value_profile is not jointly necessary.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The value_profile-axis claim that CMP_MAP gradient retention of partial-PRAGMA corpus is jointly necessary rests on inference from feedback mechanics, not a direct observation of a retained partial-keyword stepping-stone seed.

signature.mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP records a Hamming/prefix-distance bucket per CMP; an input whose keyword operand is closer to 'PRAGMA' than any seen before is preserved as corpus, supplying the I2S mutator with better substitution seeds that cmplog's edge-only feedback cannot retain.
signature.one_line: VP gradient retains partial-PRAGMA corpus giving I2S better substitution substrate

analysis.summary_one_line: Reaching this yy_reduce PRAGMA reduce action needs a literal 'PRAGMA' keyword token, requiring BOTH I2S substitution and CMP_MAP gradient.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Same PRAGMA-keyword reduce target, but reaching it also requires progressively closing the comparison distance on the keyword/operand bytes so a near-match input is preserved as a corpus stepping stone.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Adding value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient to cmplog gives the winner the new-CMP_MAP-bucket feedback that retains partial-keyword inputs; the winner resolves 9/10 and reaches the branch (avg hitcount on branch winner=5), whereas plain cmplog does not.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog-only loser seeds (SELEEC, ""/"") are short corrupted fragments discovered at 1-81s that block 10/10 (loser resolved=0/10 blocked=10) — without the CMP_MAP gradient cmplog cannot accrete the partial-keyword corpus needed to assemble PRAGMA.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP records a Hamming/prefix-distance bucket per CMP; an input whose operand pair is closer to matching the PRAGMA comparison adds a new bucket and is kept as corpus, supplying I2S with a better substitution substrate that cmplog alone cannot retain.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the true side of a branch at sqlite3.c:157969 inside the parser reduce function yy_reduce.
value_profile_cmplog wins both decisive pairs while cmplog-alone and value_profile-alone each block 10/10, the joint-necessity signature.
The I2S delta pair shows value_profile loses 0/10 resolved against the vpc winner.
Both winner-resolving seeds carry the literal ASCII 'PRAGMA' keyword at the input head.
The second winner seed also begins with PRAGMA.
Winner bytes at offsets 0x00-0x05 are P R A G M A while loser bytes are scattered punctuation and SELECT-family characters.
At the blocker line the winner takes the true side a few times while the loser never does.
Loser-blocking seeds discovered by cmplog and value_profile are short SELECT-family or punctuation fragments with no PRAGMA keyword.
cmplog gains I2SRandReplace, which scans the input for bytes matching one CMP operand and substitutes the other.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile's CMP_MAP preserves an input whose CMP operands are closer to matching than any previously seen pair.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile has no I2SRandReplace mutator.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating only on a literal PRAGMA keyword that value_profile_cmplog resolves but where cmplog-alone (with I2S) also resolves it at the same rate, showing value_profile is not jointly necessary.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The value_profile-axis claim that CMP_MAP gradient retention of partial-PRAGMA corpus is jointly necessary rests on inference from feedback mechanics, not a direct observation of a retained partial-keyword stepping-stone seed.
